articles.headline: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Jayanti: Nation Reflects on Constitutionalism, Social Justice, and Equality
articles.explainer:
• India observes the 134th birth anniversary of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, architect of the Indian Constitution, with renewed calls for upholding his ideals.
• Political leaders, academics, and civil society groups emphasize Ambedkar's vision for an egalitarian society free from discrimination.
• Discussions focus on the contemporary relevance of constitutional morality, social justice, and economic empowerment for marginalized communities.
• Government announces new initiatives under the 'PM-DAKSH' scheme to enhance skill development and employment opportunities for SC/ST/OBCs.
• Educational institutions across the country hold seminars and debates on Ambedkar's contributions to democracy, human rights, and legal reforms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's birth anniversary is on April 14.
He was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution.
He served as India's first Law and Justice Minister.
PM-DAKSH (Pradhan Mantri Dakshta Aur Kushalta Sampann Hitgrahi) Yojana is a skill development scheme for marginalized sections.
mainsPoints: Analyze the enduring relevance of Dr. Ambedkar's philosophy in contemporary India's pursuit of social justice and equality.
Discuss the challenges in achieving Ambedkar's vision of an egalitarian society and suggest policy measures.
Evaluate the role of constitutional provisions and welfare schemes in addressing historical injustices and promoting inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar with the Indian Constitution in the background.
articles.trivia: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award, in 1990.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties and Domestic Inflation Watch
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concludes its bi-monthly review, opting to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.50%.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing robust domestic growth with persistent global inflation pressures and geopolitical risks.
• RBI Governor emphasizes commitment to achieving the 4% inflation target while supporting economic activity.
• Liquidity measures are expected to remain accommodative, with a focus on targeted interventions.
• Experts foresee a potential rate cut later in the year, contingent on sustained disinflation and global economic stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth, especially in the context of global uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in achieving macro-economic stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Concludes: Focus on Critical Tech Transfer and Regional Security
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-US Strategic Dialogue concludes in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to deepen bilateral ties across various sectors.
• Key outcomes include enhanced cooperation on critical and emerging technologies, with specific agreements on technology transfer and joint development.
• Discussions covered regional security challenges, including developments in the Indo-Pacific, counter-terrorism, and maritime domain awareness.
• Both nations reiterated their support for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and pledged to work together on global issues like climate change and supply chain resilience.
• The dialogue sets the stage for upcoming high-level visits and further collaboration under the iCET initiative.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
keyTerms: iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies)
prelimsPoints: The iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies) was launched by India and the US in 2023.
The Indo-Pacific region is a key focus area for both India and the US.
Quad grouping includes India, US, Japan, and Australia, focusing on a free and open Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-US Strategic Dialogue in strengthening the bilateral relationship and its implications for regional stability.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of enhanced cooperation on critical technologies between India and the US.
Evaluate India's role in the Indo-Pacific region and how its partnership with the US contributes to its strategic objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and US flags displayed during a joint press conference.
articles.trivia: The US is one of India's largest trading partners and a key investor in various sectors.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: India Accelerates Towards Production Targets, New PLI Schemes Eyed
articles.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission shows significant progress, with several large-scale pilot projects nearing completion.
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is exploring additional Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes to boost domestic manufacturing of electrolyzers and related components.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Focus areas include green hydrogen applications in refining, fertilizers, steel, and mobility sectors.
• Challenges remain in scaling up infrastructure, reducing production costs, and ensuring a robust supply chain for renewable energy inputs.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The National Green Hydrogen Mission was approved by the Union Cabinet in January 2023.
The mission aims to achieve an annual green hydrogen production capacity of 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) by 2030.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
PLI schemes are designed to boost domestic manufacturing and exports.
mainsPoints: Assess the potential of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission to transform the country's energy landscape and contribute to global climate goals.
Discuss the key challenges in achieving the mission's ambitious targets and suggest policy interventions to overcome them.
Examine the role of PLI schemes and technological advancements in making green hydrogen economically viable and competitive.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating the process of green hydrogen production from renewable energy.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2070, with green hydrogen playing a crucial role in this target.

articles.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Next-Gen Navigation Satellite Launch, Enhancing India's Space Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is in the final stages of preparations for the launch of its next-generation navigation satellite, NVS-02.
• This launch is part of the NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation) program, aimed at providing accurate and real-time positioning and timing services.
• The NVS series satellites incorporate advanced features, including new signal frequencies and enhanced security measures.
• NavIC, originally designed for strategic uses, is increasingly being integrated into commercial applications like public vehicle tracking, disaster management, and precise timing.
• The mission underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in space-based navigation systems.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: NavIC is India's indigenous satellite navigation system.
It was earlier known as IRNSS (Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System).
NavIC provides two types of services: Standard Positioning Service (SPS) for civilian users and Restricted Service (RS) for authorized users.
The NVS-01 was the first of the second-generation satellites, launched in May 2023.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous satellite navigation systems like NavIC for India's security and economic development.
Analyze the advancements in ISRO's navigation satellite program and its potential impact on various sectors.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in space-based PNT services.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of an ISRO navigation satellite orbiting Earth.
articles.trivia: NavIC covers India and a region extending up to 1,500 km around its boundary.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Space Programme and Satellite Navigation Systems

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Revisit EWS Reservation Criteria Amidst Calls for Clarity
articles.explainer:
• A Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court is slated to commence hearings on petitions challenging the criteria for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) reservation.
• The petitions primarily question the income and asset-based criteria, particularly the ₹8 lakh annual income ceiling, arguing it may exclude genuinely needy individuals.
• The government defends the current criteria, citing recommendations from expert committees and the need for a clear, administrable definition.
• The Court's review will focus on ensuring the EWS reservation truly benefits the economically weakest sections without diluting the principle of equity.
• This decision could have significant implications for future reservation policies and the socio-economic landscape.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019, introduced 10% reservation for EWS.
Articles 15(6) and 16(6) were inserted into the Constitution to enable EWS reservation.
The current income ceiling for EWS reservation is ₹8 lakh per annum.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the constitutional validity and socio-economic implications of EWS reservation in India.
Discuss the challenges in defining 'economically weaker sections' and the controversies surrounding the existing criteria.
Suggest reforms to ensure that reservation policies effectively achieve their intended goal of social justice and equity.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The concept of EWS reservation was upheld by a 3:2 majority verdict of the Supreme Court in November 2022.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Yuva' Skill Development Program to Empower Rural Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship unveils 'Saksham Yuva', a comprehensive program targeting skill enhancement and vocational training for rural youth.
• The initiative aims to bridge the skill gap in emerging sectors and align with industry demands, particularly in manufacturing, digital services, and green jobs.
• It integrates with existing schemes like Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) but offers more localized, demand-driven training.
• Focus is also on entrepreneurship development, providing mentorship and access to micro-finance for budding rural enterprises.
• The program seeks to reduce rural-urban migration by creating sustainable livelihood opportunities within villages and small towns.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship is responsible for skill development initiatives.
PMKVY is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship (MSDE).
The program aims to address the challenge of unemployment and underemployment among youth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of skill development programs like 'Saksham Yuva' in harnessing India's demographic dividend and promoting inclusive growth.
Discuss the challenges in implementing skill development initiatives in rural areas and suggest strategies for effective outreach and impact.
Evaluate the role of government policies in fostering entrepreneurship among rural youth and creating sustainable livelihood opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: Young Indian villagers participating in a vocational training session.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest youth population, making skill development crucial for economic growth.

articles.headline: India's Q4 FY25 Export Performance Shows Robust Growth in Pharmaceuticals and IT Services
articles.explainer:
• India's merchandise and services exports recorded strong growth in the fourth quarter of Fiscal Year 2024-25, exceeding projections.
• The pharmaceutical sector emerged as a key driver, demonstrating resilience and competitiveness in global markets.
• IT and IT-enabled services (ITES) continued their upward trajectory, benefiting from digital transformation initiatives worldwide.
• Government's 'Make in India' and Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are credited for boosting manufacturing exports in select sectors.
• Despite global economic headwinds, India's diversified export basket and strategic trade agreements contribute to this positive outlook.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: India aims to achieve $1 trillion in merchandise exports and $1 trillion in services exports by 2030.
Pharmaceuticals and IT services are major contributors to India's export basket.
The 'Make in India' initiative promotes domestic manufacturing and exports.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's robust export performance in Q4 FY25 and its implications for economic growth and employment generation.
Discuss the role of government policies like 'Make in India' and PLI schemes in boosting India's manufacturing and service exports.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in diversifying its export basket and enhancing its global competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: Containers at a bustling port, symbolizing international trade.
articles.trivia: India is the 'pharmacy of the world', being the largest provider of generic drugs globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Foreign Trade and Balance of Payments

articles.headline: India Reiterates Strong Call for UNSC Reforms at G4 Meeting, Emphasizes Global Equity
articles.explainer:
• India, along with other G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, Japan), held a virtual meeting, reiterating their strong demand for urgent reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
• External Affairs Minister highlighted the need for the UNSC to reflect contemporary global realities and ensure equitable geographical representation.
• The G4 stressed that an expanded and more representative UNSC is crucial for effective multilateralism and addressing 21st-century challenges.
• Discussions focused on the need to overcome procedural hurdles and initiate text-based negotiations for UNSC expansion.
• India's permanent membership in a reformed UNSC is seen as essential to its growing global stature and contributions to international peace and security.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) mutually support each other's bids for permanent seats on the UNSC.
The UNSC currently has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
India has been a non-permanent member of the UNSC multiple times.
mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, with a specific focus on India's claim for permanent membership.
Discuss how the current structure of the UNSC impacts its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges.
Evaluate India's diplomatic efforts and strategies to achieve its objective of a permanent seat in a reformed UNSC.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) alongside the UN flag.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council was established in 1945 and its structure has largely remained unchanged since then.

articles.headline: Himalayan States Report Increased Glacier Melt, Urgent Adaptation Measures Needed
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences highlights an accelerated rate of glacier melt in the Indian Himalayan Region (IHR).
• The report warns of severe implications for water security, hydropower generation, and agricultural livelihoods in downstream areas.
• Experts call for urgent implementation of climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies, focusing on early warning systems and sustainable water management.
• The Union government is working with Himalayan states to develop a comprehensive action plan for climate resilience.
• International cooperation on transboundary water resources and climate research is deemed crucial for regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: The Indian Himalayan Region (IHR) is vital for water security in South Asia.
Glaciers are a major source of freshwater for many perennial rivers in India.
Climate change leads to glacier retreat, affecting river flows and increasing risks of natural disasters.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic and environmental consequences of accelerated glacier melt in the Himalayas for India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing effective climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies in the fragile Himalayan ecosystem.
Suggest a multi-faceted approach involving policy, technology, and community participation to address the impacts of glacier melt.
articles.imageDescription: Melting glacier in the Himalayas, showing exposed rock and water runoff.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas are often called the 'Third Pole' due to the vast amount of ice and snow they hold, second only to the polar regions.

articles.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Cyber Warfare Preparedness and Digital Security
articles.explainer:
• New Delhi is hosting a major international conference on 'Cyber Warfare Preparedness and Digital Security', bringing together global experts and policymakers.
• The conference addresses the escalating threats of cyber warfare, state-sponsored hacking, and critical infrastructure vulnerabilities.
• Discussions focus on developing robust national cyber security frameworks, fostering international cooperation, and building a skilled cyber workforce.
• India emphasizes its commitment to a free, open, and secure cyberspace, advocating for common global norms and responsible state behavior.
• Key outcomes include proposals for joint cyber drills and intelligence sharing agreements to counter emerging threats.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to cyber security incidents.
India's National Cyber Security Policy aims to protect information infrastructure.
Cyber warfare involves the use of cyber attacks by a nation-state to damage another nation's vital computer systems or networks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber warfare and its implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the measures taken by India to enhance its cyber security preparedness and identify existing gaps.
Evaluate the importance of international cooperation in combating transboundary cyber threats and establishing global norms for cyberspace.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized digital world map overlaid with security icons, representing global cyber threats.
articles.trivia: India's first cyber security policy was launched in 2013.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Basics of cyber security and challenges

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission Recommendations for North-Eastern States Under Review
articles.explainer:
• The Union Home Ministry is reviewing the recommendations submitted by the Delimitation Commission for several North-Eastern states.
• The exercise aims to redraw the boundaries of Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies based on the latest census data, ensuring proportional representation.
• The process has been met with mixed reactions, with some stakeholders expressing concerns over demographic changes and potential impacts on tribal representation.
• The Commission's mandate includes safeguarding the interests of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the delimitation process.
• Finalization of these recommendations is crucial for ensuring fair electoral representation and addressing long-standing demographic shifts in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Delimitation means the act or process of fixing limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies in a country or a province having a legislative body.
The Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India and works in collaboration with the Election Commission of India.
The current delimitation exercise is based on the 2001 Census data for most of India, but for some North-Eastern states, it has been delayed or based on later data due to specific circumstances.
mainsPoints: Explain the objectives and challenges of the delimitation exercise in a diverse country like India, with a special focus on the North-Eastern states.
Discuss the potential impacts of delimitation on political representation, regional identities, and socio-economic dynamics in the North-East.
Evaluate the mechanisms in place to ensure fair and equitable representation for marginalized communities during the delimitation process.
articles.imageDescription: A map of North-Eastern India with highlighted state boundaries.
articles.trivia: The orders of the Delimitation Commission have the force of law and cannot be called in question before any court.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Affordable Solar Cell Technology Announced by Indian Researchers
articles.explainer:
• Scientists at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bengaluru have announced a significant breakthrough in developing highly efficient and affordable solar cell technology.
• The new technology utilizes novel perovskite materials, promising higher energy conversion efficiency at a fraction of the cost of traditional silicon-based cells.
• This innovation has the potential to accelerate India's transition to renewable energy and enhance energy independence.
• Researchers are now working on scaling up production and improving the long-term stability of these next-generation solar cells.
• The development aligns with India's ambitious targets for solar energy deployment under its climate action commitments.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Perovskite solar cells are a type of solar cell that includes a perovskite-structured compound as the light-harvesting active layer.
IISc Bengaluru is a premier research institution in India.
India's National Solar Mission aims to promote solar power generation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of technological breakthroughs in solar cell efficiency and affordability for India's renewable energy goals and climate commitments.
Analyze the potential impact of perovskite solar cell technology on the Indian energy sector and its competitiveness in the global market.
Examine the role of indigenous research and development in achieving energy security and fostering innovation in India.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat examining a solar cell prototype.
articles.trivia: India has the fourth-largest installed renewable energy capacity in the world.

articles.headline: Revival of Traditional Indian Textiles: Government Initiatives and Market Trends
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles launches new schemes to promote and revive traditional Indian handlooms and handicrafts, fostering rural livelihoods.
• Focus is on providing design innovation, market linkages, and technological upgrades to artisans, especially women weavers.
• Growing consumer demand for sustainable and ethically produced fashion is driving interest in traditional textiles both domestically and internationally.
• E-commerce platforms and fashion designers are playing a crucial role in showcasing and marketing unique Indian textile traditions.
• Challenges include intellectual property protection, competition from mass-produced goods, and ensuring fair wages for artisans.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: India has a rich heritage of diverse traditional textiles like Banarasi, Kanjeevaram, Pashmina, Ikat, Kalamkari, etc.
The Ministry of Textiles is the nodal ministry for promotion of textiles and handicrafts.
GI tags help protect the intellectual property rights of traditional products.
mainsPoints: Analyze the cultural, economic, and social significance of traditional Indian textiles and handicrafts.
Discuss the government's role in promoting the revival of these crafts and empowering artisans, especially women.
Examine the challenges faced by the handloom and handicraft sectors in the modern era and suggest strategies for their sustainable growth and global competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: A skilled Indian artisan weaving intricate patterns on a handloom.
articles.trivia: India is one of the oldest and largest producers of textiles in the world.

articles.headline: Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) Allocations Increased for FY26 to Boost Rural Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Finance Ministry announces a significant increase in allocations for the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) for Fiscal Year 2025-26.
• The enhanced funding aims to accelerate the development of critical rural infrastructure projects, including irrigation, roads, bridges, and social infrastructure.
• This move is expected to stimulate agricultural growth, generate rural employment, and improve connectivity in underserved areas.
• RIDF, managed by NABARD, plays a pivotal role in channeling funds for state government projects that lack adequate budget provisions.
• The increased allocation reflects the government's continued focus on rural development as a cornerstone of inclusive economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government Budgeting
keyTerms: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development)
prelimsPoints: RIDF was constituted in 1995-96 with the primary objective of providing funds to State Governments and State-owned corporations to complete ongoing rural infrastructure projects.
NABARD is the nodal agency for managing RIDF.
The fund primarily supports projects related to irrigation, rural roads, bridges, and social sector infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) in fostering rural development and agricultural growth in India.
Analyze the impact of increased investment in rural infrastructure on employment generation, poverty reduction, and regional disparities.
Evaluate the challenges in effective utilization of RIDF funds and suggest measures to enhance project implementation and monitoring.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed rural road winding through agricultural fields.
articles.trivia: NABARD was established on July 12, 1982, as a statutory body to promote sustainable and equitable agriculture and rural prosperity.

articles.headline: Lokpal Report Highlights Need for Stronger Anti-Corruption Mechanisms and Transparency
articles.explainer:
• The annual report of the Lokpal of India, submitted to the President, underscores the persistent challenge of corruption and calls for strengthening anti-corruption frameworks.
• The report details the number of complaints received, cases investigated, and recommendations made to various government departments.
• It emphasizes the importance of enhanced transparency in public administration and proactive disclosure of information.
• The Lokpal suggests measures to expedite investigations, improve vigilance, and foster a culture of integrity across all levels of governance.
• The findings reiterate the critical role of independent oversight bodies in promoting good governance and accountability.
gsSubTopics: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, provides for the establishment of a Lokpal for the Union and Lokayuktas for States.
The Lokpal is an anti-corruption authority or body of ombudsman who represents the public interest.
The Lokpal has jurisdiction over public servants, including the Prime Minister (with certain safeguards).
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the Lokpal as an institution in combating corruption and promoting good governance in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Lokpal in fulfilling its mandate and suggest measures to strengthen its autonomy and powers.
Evaluate the importance of a comprehensive anti-corruption framework, including legal, institutional, and societal measures, for a transparent democracy.
articles.imageDescription: The emblem of the Lokpal of India.
articles.trivia: The concept of an ombudsman was first introduced in Sweden in the early 19th century.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Gains Momentum
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage is successfully attracting significant investments and accelerating domestic manufacturing.
• Several major Indian and international players have committed to setting up large-scale ACC battery manufacturing facilities under the scheme.
• This initiative is crucial for India's electric vehicle (EV) mission, renewable energy integration, and achieving energy storage independence.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's reliance on imports for critical battery components and foster a robust local supply chain.
• Challenges include securing raw material supplies, developing indigenous cell technologies, and ensuring adequate charging infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing was approved in May 2021 with an outlay of ₹18,100 crore.
ACC batteries are the new generation of advanced storage technologies that can store electric energy as electrochemical or chemical energy.
The scheme aims to establish a cumulative 50 GWh of ACC manufacturing capacity in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing in achieving India's energy transition and electric vehicle goals.
Discuss the economic and environmental benefits of localizing ACC battery production and reducing import dependence.
Examine the challenges in developing a robust ACC battery ecosystem in India, including raw material sourcing and technological development.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing various types of advanced chemistry cells (batteries).
articles.trivia: India is one of the fastest-growing markets for electric vehicles globally.

articles.headline: Wetlands Conservation: New Guidelines Issued for Ramsar Sites in India to Enhance Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change issues updated guidelines for the management and conservation of Ramsar Sites across India.
• The new framework emphasizes a holistic, ecosystem-based approach, involving local communities in wetland management plans.
• It mandates stricter monitoring of ecological health, control of pollution, and restoration of degraded wetland areas.
• India, a signatory to the Ramsar Convention, has significantly increased its number of Ramsar Sites in recent years, highlighting its commitment to wetland protection.
• The guidelines aim to ensure the ecological character of these vital ecosystems is maintained, supporting biodiversity and providing essential services.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
India is a party to the Ramsar Convention since 1982.
Ramsar Sites are wetlands designated to be of international importance under the Ramsar Convention.
The Montreux Record is a register of Ramsar sites where changes in ecological character have occurred, are occurring, or are likely to occur.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze the effectiveness of the Ramsar Convention and national policies in promoting wetland conservation in India.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including community participation and inter-sectoral coordination, for the sustainable management of Ramsar Sites.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Ramsar Convention.

articles.headline: India and Africa: Deepening Ties Through Trade and Capacity Building Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• India reiterates its commitment to strengthening its partnership with African nations, focusing on enhanced trade, investment, and capacity-building programs.
• A high-level delegation from the Ministry of External Affairs concludes a tour of several African countries, signing agreements on economic cooperation and technical assistance.
• Focus areas include infrastructure development, digital transformation, healthcare, and education, aligning with Africa's Agenda 2063.
• India offers concessional lines of credit, grants, and training programs to support Africa's developmental aspirations.
• The partnership is based on a South-South cooperation model, emphasizing mutual respect and shared growth.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and African nations share historical ties and a commitment to South-South cooperation.

articles.headline: Government Finalizes National Data Governance Policy Framework to Enhance Data Utilization and Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announces the finalization of India's National Data Governance Policy Framework.
• The policy aims to standardize data collection, storage, management, and utilization across government entities while ensuring data privacy and security.
• It emphasizes promoting data sharing, interoperability, and the creation of a robust data ecosystem for public good and economic growth.
• A dedicated India Data Management Office (IDMO) will be established to oversee the implementation of the policy and develop data standards.
• The framework is expected to boost data-driven decision-making, innovation, and the development of AI-powered services.

India has also been working on a Digital Personal Data Protection Bill.

Discuss the challenges in balancing data utilization for public good with individual privacy and security concerns.
Evaluate the role of institutional mechanisms like the India Data Management Office (IDMO) in effective data governance and policy implementation.

articles.trivia: India is one of the largest and fastest-growing digital economies in the world.

articles.headline: India Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives to Combat Extreme Weather Impacts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare rolls out enhanced initiatives to promote climate-resilient agriculture practices across vulnerable regions.
• Focus areas include developing drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and improved weather forecasting services for farmers.
• The program integrates traditional knowledge with modern scientific approaches to build farmer capacity for adapting to changing climate patterns.
• Subsidies and incentives are offered for adopting sustainable farming practices, such as organic farming and agroforestry.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard food security and farmer livelihoods against increasing instances of extreme weather events.

Initiatives like the National Innovations in Climate Resilient Agriculture (NICRA) are already in place.
Crop diversification and adoption of stress-tolerant varieties are key strategies.

Discuss the various strategies and government initiatives for promoting climate-resilient agriculture in India.
Evaluate the role of technology, traditional knowledge, and policy support in building a sustainable and adaptive agricultural sector.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers inspecting healthy crops in a field, with advanced irrigation systems visible.
articles.trivia: Agriculture is the largest employer in India, with over 50% of the population dependent on it.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting Addresses Global Economic Stability
articles.explainer:
• The G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting concludes, focusing on strategies to ensure global economic stability amidst persistent inflation and geopolitical tensions.
• Discussions covered coordinated fiscal and monetary policies, debt sustainability for low-income countries, and strengthening the international financial architecture.
• India, as a significant emerging economy, advocated for inclusive growth, climate finance, and reforms in multilateral development banks.
• The meeting emphasized the need for continued international cooperation to tackle cross-border challenges like supply chain disruptions and financial vulnerabilities.
• A joint communiqué highlights commitments to address inflation, promote sustainable finance, and support economic recovery.

Discuss India's priorities and contributions to the G20 agenda, especially concerning inclusive growth and climate finance.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
3. The mission aims to achieve an annual green hydrogen production capacity of 10 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) by 2030.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is correct: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy. Statement 3 is incorrect: The mission aims to achieve an annual green hydrogen production capacity of 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) by 2030, not 10 MMT.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body constituted under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding NavIC:
1. It is India's indigenous satellite navigation system.
2. It provides services globally, similar to GPS.
3. It was formerly known as IRNSS.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation) is indeed India's indigenous satellite navigation system. Statement 2 is incorrect: NavIC primarily provides coverage over India and a region extending up to 1,500 km around its boundary, making it a regional, not global, system unlike GPS. Statement 3 is correct: NavIC was formerly known as IRNSS (Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System).

prelimsMocks.question: The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019, introduced 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) by inserting which of the following Articles into the Constitution?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019, inserted Articles 15(6) and 16(6) in the Constitution to provide for 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) in government jobs and educational institutions.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the nodal agency for managing the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF)?
options: (b) National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)
(d) Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI)
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) was constituted in 1995-96 and is managed by NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development).

GS1.headline: Himalayan States Report Increased Glacier Melt, Urgent Adaptation Measures Needed
GS1.explainer:
• A new report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences highlights an accelerated rate of glacier melt in the Indian Himalayan Region (IHR).
• The report warns of severe implications for water security, hydropower generation, and agricultural livelihoods in downstream areas.
• Experts call for urgent implementation of climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies, focusing on early warning systems and sustainable water management.
• The Union government is working with Himalayan states to develop a comprehensive action plan for climate resilience.
• International cooperation on transboundary water resources and climate research is deemed crucial for regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.

prelimsPoints: The Indian Himalayan Region (IHR) is vital for water security in South Asia.
Glaciers are a major source of freshwater for many perennial rivers in India.
Climate change leads to glacier retreat, affecting river flows and increasing risks of natural disasters.

GS1.headline: Revival of Traditional Indian Textiles: Government Initiatives and Market Trends
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles launches new schemes to promote and revive traditional Indian handlooms and handicrafts, fostering rural livelihoods.
• Focus is on providing design innovation, market linkages, and technological upgrades to artisans, especially women weavers.
• Growing consumer demand for sustainable and ethically produced fashion is driving interest in traditional textiles both domestically and internationally.
• E-commerce platforms and fashion designers are playing a crucial role in showcasing and marketing unique Indian textile traditions.
• Challenges include intellectual property protection, competition from mass-produced goods, and ensuring fair wages for artisans.

GS2.headline: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Jayanti: Nation Reflects on Constitutionalism, Social Justice, and Equality
GS2.explainer:
• India observes the 134th birth anniversary of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, architect of the Indian Constitution, with renewed calls for upholding his ideals.
• Political leaders, academics, and civil society groups emphasize Ambedkar's vision for an egalitarian society free from discrimination.
• Discussions focus on the contemporary relevance of constitutional morality, social justice, and economic empowerment for marginalized communities.
• Government announces new initiatives under the 'PM-DAKSH' scheme to enhance skill development and employment opportunities for SC/ST/OBCs.
• Educational institutions across the country hold seminars and debates on Ambedkar's contributions to democracy, human rights, and legal reforms.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Revisit EWS Reservation Criteria Amidst Calls for Clarity
GS2.explainer:
• A Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court is slated to commence hearings on petitions challenging the criteria for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) reservation.
• The petitions primarily question the income and asset-based criteria, particularly the ₹8 lakh annual income ceiling, arguing it may exclude genuinely needy individuals.
• The government defends the current criteria, citing recommendations from expert committees and the need for a clear, administrable definition.
• The Court's review will focus on ensuring the EWS reservation truly benefits the economically weakest sections without diluting the principle of equity.
• This decision could have significant implications for future reservation policies and the socio-economic landscape.

prelimsPoints: The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019, introduced 10% reservation for EWS.
Articles 15(6) and 16(6) were inserted into the Constitution to enable EWS reservation.
The current income ceiling for EWS reservation is ₹8 lakh per annum.

Discuss the challenges in defining 'economically weaker sections' and the controversies surrounding the existing criteria.
Suggest reforms to ensure that reservation policies effectively achieve their intended goal of social justice and equity.
GS2.trivia: The concept of EWS reservation was upheld by a 3:2 majority verdict of the Supreme Court in November 2022.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Yuva' Skill Development Program to Empower Rural Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship unveils 'Saksham Yuva', a comprehensive program targeting skill enhancement and vocational training for rural youth.
• The initiative aims to bridge the skill gap in emerging sectors and align with industry demands, particularly in manufacturing, digital services, and green jobs.
• It integrates with existing schemes like Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) but offers more localized, demand-driven training.
• Focus is also on entrepreneurship development, providing mentorship and access to micro-finance for budding rural enterprises.
• The program seeks to reduce rural-urban migration by creating sustainable livelihood opportunities within villages and small towns.

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Strong Call for UNSC Reforms at G4 Meeting, Emphasizes Global Equity
GS2.explainer:
• India, along with other G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, Japan), held a virtual meeting, reiterating their strong demand for urgent reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
• External Affairs Minister highlighted the need for the UNSC to reflect contemporary global realities and ensure equitable geographical representation.
• The G4 stressed that an expanded and more representative UNSC is crucial for effective multilateralism and addressing 21st-century challenges.
• Discussions focused on the need to overcome procedural hurdles and initiate text-based negotiations for UNSC expansion.
• India's permanent membership in a reformed UNSC is seen as essential to its growing global stature and contributions to international peace and security.

GS2.imageDescription: Flags of G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) alongside the UN flag.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission Recommendations for North-Eastern States Under Review
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Home Ministry is reviewing the recommendations submitted by the Delimitation Commission for several North-Eastern states.
• The exercise aims to redraw the boundaries of Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies based on the latest census data, ensuring proportional representation.
• The process has been met with mixed reactions, with some stakeholders expressing concerns over demographic changes and potential impacts on tribal representation.
• The Commission's mandate includes safeguarding the interests of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the delimitation process.
• Finalization of these recommendations is crucial for ensuring fair electoral representation and addressing long-standing demographic shifts in the region.

prelimsPoints: Delimitation means the act or process of fixing limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies in a country or a province having a legislative body.
The Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India and works in collaboration with the Election Commission of India.
The current delimitation exercise is based on the 2001 Census data for most of India, but for some North-Eastern states, it has been delayed or based on later data due to specific circumstances.

GS2.trivia: The orders of the Delimitation Commission have the force of law and cannot be called in question before any court.

GS2.headline: Lokpal Report Highlights Need for Stronger Anti-Corruption Mechanisms and Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• The annual report of the Lokpal of India, submitted to the President, underscores the persistent challenge of corruption and calls for strengthening anti-corruption frameworks.
• The report details the number of complaints received, cases investigated, and recommendations made to various government departments.
• It emphasizes the importance of enhanced transparency in public administration and proactive disclosure of information.
• The Lokpal suggests measures to expedite investigations, improve vigilance, and foster a culture of integrity across all levels of governance.
• The findings reiterate the critical role of independent oversight bodies in promoting good governance and accountability.

GS2.headline: Government Finalizes National Data Governance Policy Framework to Enhance Data Utilization and Privacy
GS2.explainer:

• The framework is expected to boost data-driven decision-making, innovation, and the development of AI-powered services.

• India, as a significant emerging economy, advocated for inclusive growth, climate finance, and reforms in multilateral development banks.
• The meeting emphasized the need for continued international cooperation to tackle cross-border challenges like supply chain disruptions and financial vulnerabilities.
• A joint communiqué highlights commitments to address inflation, promote sustainable finance, and support economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
It works to address major issues related to the global economy, such as international financial stability, climate change mitigation, and sustainable development.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the G20 in fostering global economic stability and cooperation, particularly in the context of current economic challenges.
Discuss India's priorities and contributions to the G20 agenda, especially concerning inclusive growth and climate finance.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international forums like the G20 in addressing complex global issues that require coordinated action.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of G20 member countries arranged in a meeting setting.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties and Domestic Inflation Watch
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concludes its bi-monthly review, opting to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.50%.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing robust domestic growth with persistent global inflation pressures and geopolitical risks.
• RBI Governor emphasizes commitment to achieving the 4% inflation target while supporting economic activity.
• Liquidity measures are expected to remain accommodative, with a focus on targeted interventions.
• Experts foresee a potential rate cut later in the year, contingent on sustained disinflation and global economic stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth, especially in the context of global uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in achieving macro-economic stability in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: India Accelerates Towards Production Targets, New PLI Schemes Eyed
GS3.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission shows significant progress, with several large-scale pilot projects nearing completion.
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is exploring additional Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes to boost domestic manufacturing of electrolyzers and related components.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Focus areas include green hydrogen applications in refining, fertilizers, steel, and mobility sectors.
• Challenges remain in scaling up infrastructure, reducing production costs, and ensuring a robust supply chain for renewable energy inputs.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The National Green Hydrogen Mission was approved by the Union Cabinet in January 2023.
The mission aims to achieve an annual green hydrogen production capacity of 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) by 2030.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
PLI schemes are designed to boost domestic manufacturing and exports.
mainsPoints: Assess the potential of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission to transform the country's energy landscape and contribute to global climate goals.
Discuss the key challenges in achieving the mission's ambitious targets and suggest policy interventions to overcome them.
Examine the role of PLI schemes and technological advancements in making green hydrogen economically viable and competitive.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating the process of green hydrogen production from renewable energy.
GS3.trivia: India aims to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2070, with green hydrogen playing a crucial role in this target.

GS3.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Next-Gen Navigation Satellite Launch, Enhancing India's Space Capabilities
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is in the final stages of preparations for the launch of its next-generation navigation satellite, NVS-02.
• This launch is part of the NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation) program, aimed at providing accurate and real-time positioning and timing services.
• The NVS series satellites incorporate advanced features, including new signal frequencies and enhanced security measures.
• NavIC, originally designed for strategic uses, is increasingly being integrated into commercial applications like public vehicle tracking, disaster management, and precise timing.
• The mission underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in space-based navigation systems.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: NavIC is India's indigenous satellite navigation system.
It was earlier known as IRNSS (Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System).
NavIC provides two types of services: Standard Positioning Service (SPS) for civilian users and Restricted Service (RS) for authorized users.
The NVS-01 was the first of the second-generation satellites, launched in May 2023.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous satellite navigation systems like NavIC for India's security and economic development.
Analyze the advancements in ISRO's navigation satellite program and its potential impact on various sectors.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in space-based PNT services.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of an ISRO navigation satellite orbiting Earth.
GS3.trivia: NavIC covers India and a region extending up to 1,500 km around its boundary.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Space Programme and Satellite Navigation Systems

GS3.headline: India's Q4 FY25 Export Performance Shows Robust Growth in Pharmaceuticals and IT Services
GS3.explainer:
• India's merchandise and services exports recorded strong growth in the fourth quarter of Fiscal Year 2024-25, exceeding projections.
• The pharmaceutical sector emerged as a key driver, demonstrating resilience and competitiveness in global markets.
• IT and IT-enabled services (ITES) continued their upward trajectory, benefiting from digital transformation initiatives worldwide.
• Government's 'Make in India' and Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are credited for boosting manufacturing exports in select sectors.
• Despite global economic headwinds, India's diversified export basket and strategic trade agreements contribute to this positive outlook.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: India aims to achieve $1 trillion in merchandise exports and $1 trillion in services exports by 2030.
Pharmaceuticals and IT services are major contributors to India's export basket.
The 'Make in India' initiative promotes domestic manufacturing and exports.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's robust export performance in Q4 FY25 and its implications for economic growth and employment generation.
Discuss the role of government policies like 'Make in India' and PLI schemes in boosting India's manufacturing and service exports.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in diversifying its export basket and enhancing its global competitiveness.
GS3.imageDescription: Containers at a bustling port, symbolizing international trade.
GS3.trivia: India is the 'pharmacy of the world', being the largest provider of generic drugs globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Foreign Trade and Balance of Payments

GS3.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Cyber Warfare Preparedness and Digital Security
GS3.explainer:
• New Delhi is hosting a major international conference on 'Cyber Warfare Preparedness and Digital Security', bringing together global experts and policymakers.
• The conference addresses the escalating threats of cyber warfare, state-sponsored hacking, and critical infrastructure vulnerabilities.
• Discussions focus on developing robust national cyber security frameworks, fostering international cooperation, and building a skilled cyber workforce.
• India emphasizes its commitment to a free, open, and secure cyberspace, advocating for common global norms and responsible state behavior.
• Key outcomes include proposals for joint cyber drills and intelligence sharing agreements to counter emerging threats.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to cyber security incidents.
India's National Cyber Security Policy aims to protect information infrastructure.
Cyber warfare involves the use of cyber attacks by a nation-state to damage another nation's vital computer systems or networks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber warfare and its implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the measures taken by India to enhance its cyber security preparedness and identify existing gaps.
Evaluate the importance of international cooperation in combating transboundary cyber threats and establishing global norms for cyberspace.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized digital world map overlaid with security icons, representing global cyber threats.
GS3.trivia: India's first cyber security policy was launched in 2013.

prelimsSyllabus.topic: Basics of cyber security and challenges

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Affordable Solar Cell Technology Announced by Indian Researchers
GS3.explainer:
• Scientists at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bengaluru have announced a significant breakthrough in developing highly efficient and affordable solar cell technology.
• The new technology utilizes novel perovskite materials, promising higher energy conversion efficiency at a fraction of the cost of traditional silicon-based cells.
• This innovation has the potential to accelerate India's transition to renewable energy and enhance energy independence.
• Researchers are now working on scaling up production and improving the long-term stability of these next-generation solar cells.
• The development aligns with India's ambitious targets for solar energy deployment under its climate action commitments.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Perovskite solar cells are a type of solar cell that includes a perovskite-structured compound as the light-harvesting active layer.
IISc Bengaluru is a premier research institution in India.
India's National Solar Mission aims to promote solar power generation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of technological breakthroughs in solar cell efficiency and affordability for India's renewable energy goals and climate commitments.
Analyze the potential impact of perovskite solar cell technology on the Indian energy sector and its competitiveness in the global market.
Examine the role of indigenous research and development in achieving energy security and fostering innovation in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat examining a solar cell prototype.
GS3.trivia: India has the fourth-largest installed renewable energy capacity in the world.

GS3.headline: Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) Allocations Increased for FY26 to Boost Rural Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Finance Ministry announces a significant increase in allocations for the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) for Fiscal Year 2025-26.
• The enhanced funding aims to accelerate the development of critical rural infrastructure projects, including irrigation, roads, bridges, and social infrastructure.
• This move is expected to stimulate agricultural growth, generate rural employment, and improve connectivity in underserved areas.
• RIDF, managed by NABARD, plays a pivotal role in channeling funds for state government projects that lack adequate budget provisions.
• The increased allocation reflects the government's continued focus on rural development as a cornerstone of inclusive economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government Budgeting
keyTerms: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development)
prelimsPoints: RIDF was constituted in 1995-96 with the primary objective of providing funds to State Governments and State-owned corporations to complete ongoing rural infrastructure projects.
NABARD is the nodal agency for managing RIDF.
The fund primarily supports projects related to irrigation, rural roads, bridges, and social sector infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) in fostering rural development and agricultural growth in India.
Analyze the impact of increased investment in rural infrastructure on employment generation, poverty reduction, and regional disparities.
Evaluate the challenges in effective utilization of RIDF funds and suggest measures to enhance project implementation and monitoring.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed rural road winding through agricultural fields.
GS3.trivia: NABARD was established on July 12, 1982, as a statutory body to promote sustainable and equitable agriculture and rural prosperity.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Gains Momentum
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage is successfully attracting significant investments and accelerating domestic manufacturing.
• Several major Indian and international players have committed to setting up large-scale ACC battery manufacturing facilities under the scheme.
• This initiative is crucial for India's electric vehicle (EV) mission, renewable energy integration, and achieving energy storage independence.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's reliance on imports for critical battery components and foster a robust local supply chain.
• Challenges include securing raw material supplies, developing indigenous cell technologies, and ensuring adequate charging infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing was approved in May 2021 with an outlay of ₹18,100 crore.
ACC batteries are the new generation of advanced storage technologies that can store electric energy as electrochemical or chemical energy.
The scheme aims to establish a cumulative 50 GWh of ACC manufacturing capacity in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing in achieving India's energy transition and electric vehicle goals.
Discuss the economic and environmental benefits of localizing ACC battery production and reducing import dependence.
Examine the challenges in developing a robust ACC battery ecosystem in India, including raw material sourcing and technological development.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing various types of advanced chemistry cells (batteries).
GS3.trivia: India is one of the fastest-growing markets for electric vehicles globally.

GS3.headline: Wetlands Conservation: New Guidelines Issued for Ramsar Sites in India to Enhance Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change issues updated guidelines for the management and conservation of Ramsar Sites across India.
• The new framework emphasizes a holistic, ecosystem-based approach, involving local communities in wetland management plans.
• It mandates stricter monitoring of ecological health, control of pollution, and restoration of degraded wetland areas.
• India, a signatory to the Ramsar Convention, has significantly increased its number of Ramsar Sites in recent years, highlighting its commitment to wetland protection.
• The guidelines aim to ensure the ecological character of these vital ecosystems is maintained, supporting biodiversity and providing essential services.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
India is a party to the Ramsar Convention since 1982.
Ramsar Sites are wetlands designated to be of international importance under the Ramsar Convention.
The Montreux Record is a register of Ramsar sites where changes in ecological character have occurred, are occurring, or are likely to occur.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze the effectiveness of the Ramsar Convention and national policies in promoting wetland conservation in India.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including community participation and inter-sectoral coordination, for the sustainable management of Ramsar Sites.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
GS3.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Ramsar Convention.

GS3.headline: India Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives to Combat Extreme Weather Impacts
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare rolls out enhanced initiatives to promote climate-resilient agriculture practices across vulnerable regions.
• Focus areas include developing drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and improved weather forecasting services for farmers.
• The program integrates traditional knowledge with modern scientific approaches to build farmer capacity for adapting to changing climate patterns.
• Subsidies and incentives are offered for adopting sustainable farming practices, such as organic farming and agroforestry.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard food security and farmer livelihoods against increasing instances of extreme weather events.

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

keyTerms: National Adaptation Fund for Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture.
Initiatives like the National Innovations in Climate Resilient Agriculture (NICRA) are already in place.
Crop diversification and adoption of stress-tolerant varieties are key strategies.

Discuss the various strategies and government initiatives for promoting climate-resilient agriculture in India.
Evaluate the role of technology, traditional knowledge, and policy support in building a sustainable and adaptive agricultural sector.

GS3.trivia: Agriculture is the largest employer in India, with over 50% of the population dependent on it.
